title: Build a landing page in 5 minutes
description: Build a single semantic landing page with header, hero, two feature cards, and footer. Aim for clean structure over visual polish.

starter.styles.css: body { font-family: system-ui; margin: 0; padding: 16px; background: #fff; }

title: Accessible modal markup
description: Write the static markup for an accessible modal: role=dialog, aria-modal, aria-labelledby pointing at the title, a close button labelled with aria-label.

starter.styles.css:
body { padding: 16px; }
[role=dialog] { border: 1px solid #ccc; padding: 16px; max-width: 400px; }

title: Build a responsive image grid
description: Use CSS Grid to display the cards in a responsive layout: 1 column on mobile, 2 on tablet (≥640px), 3 on desktop (≥1024px). Use a 16px gap.

.grid { /* TODO */ }
.card { background: #6366f1; color: white; padding: 32px; border-radius: 12px; text-align: center; }

title: Flatten a nested array
description: Implement `flatten(arr)` that returns a single-level array containing all elements at any depth. Cannot use Array.prototype.flat.

title: Implement throttle
description: Implement `throttle(fn, ms)` that ensures `fn` is called at most once every `ms` milliseconds. The first call should fire immediately.

tests.name: fires immediately
tests.body: let n = 0; const f = throttle(() => n++, 50); f(); expect(n).toBe(1);

tests.name: ignores within window
tests.body: let n = 0; const f = throttle(() => n++, 50); f(); f(); f(); expect(n).toBe(1);

tests.name: fires again after window
tests.body: let n = 0; const f = throttle(() => n++, 30); f(); await wait(50); f(); expect(n).toBe(2);

title: Implement Promise.all
description: Implement `promiseAll(arr)` mirroring Promise.all: resolves with an array of values when all input promises resolve, rejects on the first rejection.

tests.name: resolves with values in order
tests.body: const r = await promiseAll([Promise.resolve(1), Promise.resolve(2)]); expect(r).toEqual([1, 2]);

tests.name: rejects on first failure
tests.body: let err; try { await promiseAll([Promise.resolve(1), Promise.reject(new Error('x'))]); } catch (e) { err = e; } expect(err.message).toBe('x');

title: Build a Toggle component (5 min)
description: Build a controlled <Toggle checked onChange /> component with proper aria-pressed and keyboard support (Space/Enter).
rubric: Solution should: (1) accept checked + onChange props, (2) be a <button> with aria-pressed, (3) toggle on click and keyboard, (4) be visually styleable via classNames or data attributes.

title: Server Action with Zod & error state
description: Write a server action `createPost(prevState, formData)` that validates with Zod, returns a typed errors object on failure, and revalidates the path on success.
rubric: Solution should: (1) declare 'use server', (2) parse FormData with a Zod schema, (3) return { ok: false, errors } shape on failure, (4) revalidatePath on success, (5) be usable with useActionState.

title: Type-only: DeepReadonly
description: Define `DeepReadonly<T>` that makes every nested property readonly, including arrays.

starter.solution.ts:
// type DeepReadonly<T> = ...

function smoke() { return true; }

solution: type DeepReadonly<T> = T extends (...args: any) => any ? T : T extends object ? { readonly [K in keyof T]: DeepReadonly<T[K]> } : T;
rubric: Use a recursive mapped type with conditional handling for functions and primitives.

title: In-memory rate limiter
description: Implement `createLimiter({ max, windowMs })` returning `try(key)` that returns true if the call is allowed. Use a sliding window per key.
rubric: Solution should: (1) keep a Map<key, timestamps[]>, (2) on each call, drop timestamps older than now - windowMs, (3) allow if remaining count < max and push the current timestamp, else deny, (4) handle cleanup of empty entries.
